title: Universal Is Skipping Influencer Screenings for 'The Odyssey.' Film Critics Are Thrilled
gist: Universal Studios is departing from the industry norm of hosting influencer pre-screenings for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, instead betting on traditional press and critic cycles. Film critics view this as a rebuke of the 'gushy and uncritical' influencer marketing machine, while the film is already tracking for a blockbuster $80-100M opening weekend with record-breaking ticket presales.

insights.claim: Universal Studios has decided to skip influencer pre-screenings for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, a departure from an industry norm where influencer screenings generate effusive social media praise to cushion mixed critic reviews.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: A major studio is openly signaling that influencer-driven marketing may be less valuable for prestige tentpole releases, with implications for influencer marketing budgets across the industry.
insights.quote: Universal Studios has opted to skip the previews it typically holds for influencers. These screenings, common throughout the industry, tend to generate effusive praise on social media and fan blogs that can soften the blow of mixed or negative reviews from professional critics.

insights.claim: Film critics characterize influencer social media reactions to movies as 'total bullshit,' reflecting deep industry tension between professional criticism and paid/cultivated influencer promotion.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Growing credibility gap between influencer marketing and professional criticism could accelerate studios rethinking influencer spend for high-profile releases.
insights.quote: "GOOD!!" he wrote in a post reacting to the news on X. "Because EVERYONE knows those so-called 'influencer' social media reactions are TOTAL BULLSHIT."

insights.claim: Universal's strategy positions influencers and press critics as equals at the same screenings, which critics note is a psychologically clever move by Nolan to generate goodwill without paying for it.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Studios can gain critic goodwill simply by treating them as the primary audience, a low-cost lever that may improve review sentiment for prestige films.
insights.quote: "it was also a canny move on Nolan's part, because now whoever does get invited to a press screening—including influencers, who will see it at the same time as the rest of us—will feel like, 'Oh wow, Christopher Nolan values me as a smart, professional critic!'"

insights.claim: Skipping influencer screenings is not expected to become an industry-wide trend, but could become a niche 'stamp of quality' signal for prestige projects that studios believe stand on their own merits.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: If the strategy becomes associated with quality signaling, it could create a two-tier marketing ecosystem — influencer-heavy for mainstream blockbusters, critic-first for prestige tent-poles.
insights.quote: "It would become its own weird stamp of approval," he says. "'No, seriously, this extremely hyped film is actually good—you don't have to take the word of the influencers we helped cultivate to do our shilling for us!'"

themes.theme: The Odyssey Box Office and Presale Demand

insights.claim: The Odyssey is tracking for an $80-100 million opening weekend, with ticket presales so overwhelming that theater apps crashed and BFI Imax set a 24-hour seat-sold record for the largest screen in the UK.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Premium large-format (PLF) venues like Imax continue to command disproportionate demand for tentpole releases, underlining the value of PLF partnerships for theater operators.
insights.quote: The film is on track for a massive opening of $80 million to $100 million, and movie theater apps and websites were instantly overloaded when tickets went on sale earlier this month.

insights.claim: Scalpers listed Odyssey Imax and 70mm tickets for as much as $1,000 on eBay after premium format showings rapidly sold out across the US, signaling extreme consumer willingness to pay for exclusive theatrical experiences.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Secondary market pricing for theatrical tickets validates that premium format scarcity is a real consumer pain point — an opportunity for theaters to capture more value through dynamic pricing.
insights.quote: scalpers to list tickets for as much as $1,000 on eBay

insights.claim: The Odyssey shattered the 24-hour seats-sold record at BFI Imax, the largest screen in the UK, underscoring Christopher Nolan's unique ability to drive premium theatrical attendance.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Nolan-branded films are a rare proven driver of PLF revenue, making Universal's distribution partnership a key asset for theater chains competing against streaming.
insights.quote: It shattered the record for most seats sold in a 24-hour period at BFI Imax, which features the largest screen in the UK

insights.claim: The Odyssey has faced online backlash over its diverse casting and supposedly 'woke' interpretation of the Homer epic, a pre-release culture war controversy that Universal appears to be navigating by elevating critic credibility over influencer hot takes.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Culture war controversies around diverse casting in tentpole films have become a standard headwind that studios now appear to counter with prestige-critic strategies rather than social media engagement.
insights.quote: The ugly gutter fights are about the film's diverse casting and its supposedly "woke" slant on the source material.

insights.claim: Anti-woke critics claim Universal's decision to bypass influencers signals fear of right-wing backlash, but box office tracking and presale data show no evidence the controversy is materially impacting demand.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Culture war noise around tentpole films has historically not correlated with box office underperformance when underlying demand signals (presales, PLF sellouts) are strong — a useful investor framework.
insights.quote: there's no reason to suspect that anyone at Universal is sweating right now

themes.theme: Shift in Film Industry Power Dynamics

insights.claim: Hollywood studios have systematically used influencer screenings to diminish the importance of professional critics, using social media amplification to frame critical tentpoles as 'for the fans, not the critics.'
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: The influencer-over-critic marketing playbook has been a deliberate strategic choice by studios, not an organic evolution — meaning it can be just as deliberately reversed when brand or quality considerations warrant.
insights.quote: "Actual film critics have gotten used to how the studios try to diminish whatever importance we have by often letting influencers be the first people to sound off on a film," he says.

insights.claim: Universal's bet on a traditional publicity cycle for The Odyssey signals that for prestige auteur projects, a studio may gain more net value from organic critic consensus than from manufactured influencer hype.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Distinguishing between blockbusters that need influencer hype versus prestige films that benefit from critic legitimacy could become a more explicit marketing segmentation strategy.
insights.quote: "Essentially, the studio is declaring that it doesn't need influencers—who tend to be very gushy and uncritical—to bolster the film's initial word-of-mouth," Grierson says.

insights.claim: Critics who feel their work is respected may be marginally more inclined to write favorably about a film, creating a subtle but real incentive alignment when studios elevate critic access.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Studio-critic relations are a soft but real input to review sentiment, which in turn influences Rotten Tomatoes scores — a factor that can affect opening weekend multipliers.
insights.quote: maybe critics who feel their work is receiving the respect it deserves are slightly more inclined to write favorably of a film
